mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations and producing positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced household income, which suppresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized expansion in consumer spending and residential investment, generating positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during monetary tightening cycles reflects resilient manufacturing capacity that outpaces rate hike headwinds, yielding positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during tightening periods with rising INDPRO are negative in > 60% of observations, or signal loses predictive power after 2015.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth directly increases aggregate corporate earnings expectations, producing positive forward equity returns over quarterly investment horizons.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and triggers negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of high-inflation trigger months, or inflation surprises fail to predict return direction with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 58% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during late-cycle expansions signal weakening consumer confidence and tightening credit conditions, producing negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of declining HOUST observations during late-cycle phases, or signal accuracy drops below chance levels.
